component.name: beyond-shor.eu
component.description: Post-Quantum Cryptography blog and interactive playground

components.name: AES-256-GCM
components.description: Hybrid Encryption Playground — Authenticated symmetric encryption (AEAD, 256-bit key). Grover's algorithm halves the effective key length: AES-256 provides ~128-bit post-quantum security, corresponding to NIST Level 1.

components.name: Classic McEliece 8192128
components.description: Hybrid Encryption Playground — Code-based KEM (binary Goppa codes). Oldest and most battle-tested PQC assumption (50+ years). Very large public keys (~1 MB). Quantum-safe at NIST security level 5. NIST Round 4 alternate candidate.

components.name: FrodoKEM-1344
components.description: Hybrid Encryption Playground — Lattice-based KEM (plain LWE — no ring/module structure). Most conservative lattice assumption. Quantum-safe at NIST security level 5. NIST Round 3 alternate candidate maintained by Microsoft Research.

components.name: HKDF-SHA-256
components.description: Hybrid Encryption Playground — HMAC-based Key Derivation Function with SHA-256 (RFC 5869 / NIST SP 800-56C). Derives a combined AES-256 key from the X25519 and KEM shared secrets in the hybrid encryption playground.

components.name: HMAC-SHA-256
components.description: Contact form — HMAC with SHA-256 (NIST FIPS 198). Grover's algorithm reduces the underlying hash preimage resistance to ~128 bits. Level 2 follows CycloneDX categorisation.

components.name: HS256 (JWT)
components.description: Strapi CMS — API authentication tokens (internal, server-to-server only).

components.name: ML-DSA-65
components.description: Article signing and verification — pure ML-DSA per FIPS 204 §5.2: raw UTF-8 message bytes are passed directly, no pre-hashing. ML-DSA internally derives µ = SHAKE-256(tr ∥ M, 64). NIST FIPS 204 (Strapi lifecycle hook + browser verifier).

components.name: ML-KEM-1024
components.description: Hybrid Encryption Playground — NIST FIPS 203 (August 2024). Lattice-based KEM (Module-LWE). Quantum-safe at NIST security level 5.

components.name: SHA-256
components.description: Article signing and verification — SHA-256 (NIST FIPS 180-4). Used here to hash media file content (images) into a compact 32-byte digest that is embedded in the signed article message. Grover's algorithm reduces preimage resistance to ~128 bits. Level 2 follows the classical CycloneDX categorisation.

components.name: SHAKE-256
components.description: Article signing and verification — SHAKE-256 (NIST FIPS 202) — extendable output function (XOF) based on Keccak-1600. Used internally by ML-DSA-65 to derive the message representative µ = SHAKE-256(tr ∥ M, 64) per FIPS 204 §5.2. Not called directly in source; implicit via @noble/post-quantum.

components.name: X25519
components.description: Hybrid Encryption Playground — Classical ECDH key exchange (Curve25519). Quantum-VULNERABLE — Shor's algorithm breaks the elliptic curve discrete logarithm. Intentionally included as quantum-vulnerable baseline in hybrid schemes.
